title: [ampharos] Battle Guide
summary: A 115 Special Attack [electric]-type bulky attacker that abuses [thunder-wave] and [volt-switch] to control the pace of the match.
strengths: 115 Special Attack produces reliable breaker damage
90/85 dual bulk allows it to tank hits while attacking
[thunder-wave] breaks the opposing Speed tier for teammates
weaknesses: 55 Speed forces [ampharos] to move last against almost everything
Ground type immunity blanks the [electric] STAB

recommendedMoves.reason: Reliable [electric] STAB for main damage output

recommendedItems.reason: Boosts [electric] STAB damage without locking moves

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[ampharos] is a pure [electric] special attacker with an unusual stat distribution — 90 HP, 85 Special Defense, 115 Special Attack, and only 55 Speed. That combination reads as a slow breaker or bulky pivot rather than a traditional attacker, and that's exactly how it's used. [ampharos] trades speed for durability, takes hits from opposing special attackers, and uses [thunder-wave] plus [volt-switch] to control the pace of the match for its teammates rather than racing for KOs.

## Strengths
115 Special Attack is strong enough to pressure most walls, and [ampharos]'s bulk means it gets multiple turns of output instead of just one attack per entry. [thunderbolt] provides reliable STAB damage, and [volt-switch] turns every shaky matchup into a pivot opportunity. [thunder-wave] is the key utility move that converts [ampharos]'s slow Speed into team-wide Speed control, paralyzing faster threats and letting teammates outrun them.

## Weaknesses & Answers
55 Speed means [ampharos] almost always moves last, so any attacker threatening a one-shot removes it before it can act. The Ground immunity is the defining structural problem — any grounded Ground type walls [electric] STAB, and the narrow coverage pool means [ampharos] lacks clean answers beyond [focus-blast]. The low Speed tier also means priority attackers and faster choice-scarf users control the tempo of every [ampharos] matchup.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the locked [electric] STAB. [volt-switch] provides the pivot role and is mandatory for team tempo. [focus-blast] fills the coverage slot as the best available Fighting move, though the 70 percent accuracy is a real drawback. [thunder-wave] is the team utility slot that extends [ampharos]'s value beyond its own damage output. dragon-pulse is an alternate coverage pick for Dragon matchups.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[magnet] is the default — it boosts [electric] STAB damage with no drawback and keeps move flexibility intact. [leftovers] is an alternate for the tank build that wants passive recovery over raw damage. The ability is [static] by default — plus is only useful in doubles, so it's a dead slot in singles. Tera [electric] maxes [thunderbolt] damage, while Tera [fairy] flips the Dragon resistances into a strong defensive profile.

## Teammates & Synergy
The biggest structural need is a Ground answer — a Grass or Flying partner ensures the [electric] STAB stays relevant. A tailwind setter or screens user accelerates [ampharos]'s slow pace, and a physical wall covers the weakness to physical breakers. Fast cleaners on the back line convert [ampharos]'s [thunder-wave] pressure into wins, and a hazard remover preserves the bulk over a long match.

## Play Patterns
Enter on predicted [electric]-absorb targets or on a safe pivot, use [thunder-wave] against faster attackers, then [volt-switch] out to a faster teammate. Against bulky walls, use [focus-blast] as the coverage pick. Save [thunderbolt] for predictable flying or water matchups where STAB lands for full damage. Save the Tera specifically for a Ground attack — using it defensively almost always preserves [ampharos] for the late game.
